skill.name: evaluation
skill.description: Design evaluation frameworks for agent systems using rubrics, test sets, LLM-as-judge workflows, quality gates, and production monitoring.
skill.summary: Build practical evaluation systems for agent quality, regressions, and production performance.
skill.author: ChakshuGautam

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, key material, and shell execution patterns, but review found false positives, leaving only a low script-presence risk. The flagged content is Markdown documentation, numeric scoring scales, dictionary key access, and a local evaluation helper script without network, subprocess, environment, or filesystem access. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious execution intent was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptography patterns are scoring values
low_findings.description: Static weak-crypto detections map to evaluation score labels, rubric weights, and version numbers. They do not call cryptographic libraries or select weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines define numeric rubric levels such as 1.0, 0.8, and 0.0. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or security-sensitive algorithm is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: shell backticks are Markdown fences
low_findings.description: Static shell-execution detections are caused by Markdown fenced code blocks in documentation. The backticks are not Ruby or shell execution syntax in runnable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detections align with Markdown code fences around explanatory examples. There is no shell invocation, command construction, or user-controlled command path.

low_findings.title: False positive: key material detections are dictionary keys
low_findings.description: Sensitive key-file detections refer to dictionary key iteration and rubric key names. No certificate, private key, secret, or credential material is included.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact contexts use Python dictionary keys for rubric dimensions. No PEM block, certificate file path, password, token, or secret value appears in the reviewed lines.

low_findings.title: Reviewed executable helper script
low_findings.description: The skill includes a Python evaluator helper, which is a normal script risk factor. Review found no subprocess, network, environment variable, or filesystem access in the script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The imports and implementation are limited to typing, dataclasses, enum, time, and local random sampling. No dangerous execution or exfiltration primitive was found during targeted review.

content.value_statement: Agent behavior is hard to judge with single tests or fixed expected paths. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code build rubrics, test sets, and quality gates.
actual_capabilities: Defines multi-dimensional rubrics for factual accuracy, completeness, citations, source quality, and tool efficiency.
Guides test set design across simple, medium, complex, and very complex agent tasks.
Explains LLM-as-judge, human review, end-state evaluation, and continuous evaluation workflows.
Provides Python helper classes for evaluator logic, test set management, runners, and monitoring summaries.
Supports regression checks and quality gates for agent pipeline changes.
limitations: The included Python evaluator uses simple heuristics and demonstration logic.
It does not provide a hosted dashboard, database integration, or CI configuration.
LLM-as-judge prompts and human review processes still need project-specific design.
Evaluation results depend on representative test cases and clear ground truth.

use_cases.title: Validate an agent release
use_cases.description: Create a rubric and test suite that catches regressions before deploying a changed agent pipeline.

use_cases.title: Compare agent configurations
use_cases.description: Score different prompts, models, tools, or context strategies using the same quality dimensions.

use_cases.title: Monitor production quality
use_cases.description: Sample real interactions and track pass rates, average scores, and alert thresholds over time.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Rubric
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new agent workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an evaluation rubric for my agent. The task is: [describe task]. Include 5 dimensions, score levels, and pass criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Test Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Create representative cases before evaluation runs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a test set for this agent workflow: [describe workflow]. Include simple, medium, complex, and edge cases with expected outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Review Evaluation Results
prompt_templates.scenario: Analyze an evaluation run and decide what to fix.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these agent evaluation results: [paste summary]. Identify failure patterns, likely causes, and the next three improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Quality Gate
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release process for agent pipelines.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a release quality gate for this agent pipeline: [describe pipeline]. Include rubric weights, thresholds, human review triggers, and monitoring metrics.

output_examples.input: Evaluate a customer support agent that must answer policy questions with citations.
output: A five-dimension rubric covering factual accuracy, citation accuracy, completeness, tone, and escalation judgment.
A test set split across routine requests, ambiguous policy cases, and high-risk edge cases.
A pass threshold with human review for low citation accuracy or policy conflicts.

output_examples.input: Compare two research agent prompts after a model upgrade.
output: A paired evaluation plan using the same tasks, rubric, token budget, and review process.
A comparison summary focused on answer accuracy, source quality, tool efficiency, and failure types.
A recommendation to ship, revise, or expand the test set before release.

output_examples.input: Set up monitoring for an agent in production.
output: A sampling strategy for real conversations with privacy-aware output previews.
Warning and critical thresholds for pass rate, average score, and repeated failure modes.
A review cadence that combines automated scoring with periodic human audits.

best_practices: Use outcome-focused rubrics because agents can solve the same task through different valid paths.
Build test sets from real user workflows, known failures, and complexity tiers.
Combine automated scoring with human review for high-impact or ambiguous cases.
anti_patterns: Do not judge agents only by exact step sequences or brittle golden paths.
Do not rely on one aggregate score without dimension-level failure analysis.
Do not tune prompts only against a small test set without checking broader regressions.

faq.question: What kind of agents does this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: It fits research, support, coding, tool-using, and workflow agents that need measurable quality checks.

faq.question: Does it replace human evaluation?
faq.answer: No. It helps automate repeatable checks, but human review is still important for edge cases and impact.

faq.question: Can it create LLM-as-judge rubrics?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes dimensions, scoring levels, judge inputs, and thresholds for structured evaluation.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Python helper script for evaluator classes, test sets, runners, and monitoring summaries.

faq.question: Can it monitor production systems?
faq.answer: It gives monitoring patterns and sample logic, but teams must connect storage, privacy controls, and dashboards.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare representative tasks, expected outcomes when available, business priorities, and examples of past failures.
